info.title: How to manage systems and packages in &suselinux; 16

abstract.para: &suselinux; 16 replaces &yast; with a combination of &cockpit; for interactive management and &ansible; or &smlm; for automation, while also simplifying the package and update stack.

section.title: &cockpit; replaces &yast; for 1:1 management
&ansible; and &smlm; for automation
section.para: &suselinux; 16 supports &ansible; for automation. &smlm;, which uses Salt internally, is also available for managing &suselinux; 16 installations. Note that &suselinux; 16 itself does not include Salt packages.
WBEM support (previously provided by SBLIM packages in &slea; 15) has been dropped.

section.title: How has package management and the update stack changed?
section.para: &suselinux; 16 removes the module concept used in &slea; 15 (base system, server applications, development packages, and so on). Separate pool and update channels are also gone. These changes, combined with &zypper; improvements, deliver better update performance.
Minor releases (16.1, 16.2, and so on) continue to use separate repositories.
